title: scRNAseq analysis
text: The Parse pipeline handles the initial processing of combinatorial indexing single-cell gene expression data. It takes raw FASTQs and outputs a Digital Gene Expression (DGE) matrix.

title: scRNAseq analysis
text:

Because the data comes in multiple lanes, we concatenate them:

title: scRNAseq analysis
text:
Known Issue
There wass a clash between the snakemake environment and the spipe environment. Workaround: We manually activate spipe-1.3.1 within the shell block rather than using the conda: directive. :::

title: Project Overview
text:
sn-eQTL Analysis Workflow





Workflow Summary
The pipeline is divided into 13 main modules: 1. 01 Parse: Raw data processing and alignment. 2. 02 Scanpy: snRNAseq QC and clustering 3. 03 Genotyping (pre-imputation): Genotype QC and harmonisation for TOPMED imputation 4. 04 Genotyping (post-imputation): Genotype QC post-imputation 5. 05 TensorQTL: eQTL Discovery and QC 6. 06 eQTL replication: pi1 Testing etc. 7. 07 Prep GWAS: Download and munge GWAS data for downstream applications 8. 08 SuSiE finemapping: Generate finemapped SNPs for MaxCPP annotation 9. 09 S-LDSR: Run Stratified LDSR 10. 10 SMR: Run Summary Mendelian Randomisation 11. 11 TWAS-weights: Generate TWAS weights for cTWAS 12. 12 cTWAS: Run Causal TWAS 13. 12 Visualisation: Generate figures and tables for manuscript

title: Parse Alignment
text:
Overview
This pipeline performs combinatorial indexing processing using the Parse Biosciences split-pipe (v1.3.1) software. It orchestrates the transition from raw FASTQ multiplexed libraries to a Digital Gene Expression (DGE) matrix.



Workflow Logic
The pipeline follows a tiered execution strategy to manage heavy I/O and storage constraints.





graph TD
    A[get_refs] --> B[make_ref]
    C[cat_fqs] --> D[run_parse]
    B --> D
    D --> E[run_parse_combine]

    style A fill:#f96,stroke:#333
    style B fill:#f96,stroke:#333
    style C fill:#f96,stroke:#333
    style D fill:#f96,stroke:#333
    style E fill:#f96,stroke:#333







get_refs: Download the hg38 reference files (Ensembl Release 113).
make_ref: Prepares genome indices for split-pipe.
cat_fqs: Combine fastq files per sublibrary (across lanes), per read (R1, R2).
run_parse: Core alignment and barcode demultiplexing.
run_parse_combine: Aggregates sublibraries into a unified plate-level DGE.








Warning



Environment Clash Workaround
There was a conflict between the Snakemake YAML parser and the spipe environment. Fix: The spipe-1.3.1 environment is manually activated within the shell block of the rule rather than via the Snakemake conda: directive.





Technical Requirements



Category
Requirement




Software
Parse split-pipe v1.3.1


Environment
spipe-1.3.1


Reference
ENSEMBL Release 113 (hg38)


Inputs
Demultiplexed FASTQs, Sample List


Metadata
JSON path map (via scripts/create_parse_json.py)


Disk Space
~3-5 TB per plate (Scratch)






Library Composition & Throughput
We processed 150 samples across three sequencing plates:







Category
Details




Sublibraries
43 total (Plate 1: 15, Plate 2: 16, Plate 3: 12)


FASTQ info
Each sublibrary consists of 24 files (4 lanes × 3 runs × 2 reads)


Data Volume
~3.0 TB of final raw output (~1 TB per plate)






Resource Management Strategy
To navigate a 5TB storage quota and heavy memory requirements, the following engineering strategies were implemented:







Category
Details




Concurrency Control
Restricted to 3 parallel jobs to prevent storage overflow


I/O Optimization
Combined FASTQ transfer and concatenation into a single step to minimize scratch disk residency


Cleanup Logic
Intermediate merged FASTQs were deleted immediately upon successful alignment


HPC Allocation
Executed on DPMCN nodes (c_compute_neuro1) to avoid prohibitive queue times on Hawk






Compute profile



Category
Resource




Threads
32


Memory
360 GB


Walltime
10 Days






Known Issue
There wass a clash between the snakemake environment and the spipe environment. Workaround: We manually activate spipe-1.3.1 within the shell block rather than using the conda directive.

title: Prepare GWAS
text:
Overview
The S-LDSR pipeline quantifies the enrichment of heritability for specific GWAS traits within the functional regions identified by our eQTL mapping. Specifically, it uses the maxCPP annotation from the SuSiE fine-mapping output to determine if cell-type-specific eQTLs are enriched for the heritability of neuropsychiatric traits.



Workflow Logic
The pipeline follows a tiered execution strategy to manage heavy I/O and storage constraints.





graph TD
    %% Define the style once
    classDef snazzy fill:#f1f1f1,stroke:#333,stroke-width:2px,color:#000;

    A[SuSiE Credible Sets] --> B{make_annot}
    C[hg38 Ref Panels] --> D[ldsr_ld_scores]
    B --> D
    E[HapMap3 SNPs] --> F[lift_hapmap3_snps]
    F --> D
    D --> G{ldsr_strat_hg38}
    H[Munged GWAS Sumstats] --> G
    G --> I[ldsr_strat_summary]
    I --> J[ldsr_report.html]

   %% Apply the style to all boxes
    class A,B,C,D,E,F,G,H,I,J snazzy







get_hg38_refs: Download SLDSR hg38 reference files.
get_hg19_refs: Download SLDSR hg38 reference files.
make_annot_maxCPP: Converts SuSiE credible sets into LDSR-compatible MaxCPP annotation files.
lift_hapmap3_snps: Lift Hapmap 3 reference SNPs to hg38 coordinates.
ldsr_ld_scores_hg38: Calculates stratified LD scores for each cell type (MaxCPP anns).
ldsr_strat_hg38_bl_v12: Run stratified LD score regression (partitioned by cell type and trait) with baseline version 1.2.
ldsr_report: Aggregates all enrichment scores into a finalized HTML report.








Note



Coordinate Consistency
LDSR was originally built using references for hg19. However, hg38 references are also avaliable and we used the latter for our analysis. Note GWA aligned to hg19 were lifted over to hg38 in the Prep GWAS pepeline.




Technical requirements



Category
Requirement




Software
LDSR (LD Score Regression v1.0.1)


Environment
ldsr (Conda) / r_eqtl (Singularity)


Reference Panels
1000 Genomes hg38 (Baseline v1.2)


Functional Inputs
SuSiE maxCPP annotations


SNP Set
HapMap3 (Lifts to hg38 via CrossMap)






GWAS Traits Tbale
GWAS data were accessed from the following locations:



Trait
Data Download
Filename




Schizophrenia (SCZ)
Link
PGC3_SCZ_wave3.european.autosome.public.v3.vcf.tsv.gz


Bipolar Disorder (BIP)
Link
bip2024_eur_no23andMe.gz


Major Depressive Disorder (MDD)
Link
pgc-mdd2025_no23andMe-noUKBB_eur_v3-49-24-11.tsv.gz


ADHD
Link
ADHD2022_iPSYCH_deCODE_PGC.meta.gz


OCD
Link
daner_OCD_full_wo23andMe_190522.gz






Implementation Detail
The pipeline uses eval to hook the Conda environment within the shell block. This ensures that the ldsc Python 2.7 environment (required by the original software) is correctly isolated from the Snakemake execution environment.
# Environment Activation Pattern
eval "$(/apps/languages/miniforge3/24.3.0-0/bin/conda shell.bash hook)"
conda activate ldsr



Run Composition & Throughput
The S-LDSC pipeline iterates across chromosomes, cell types, functional annotations, and GWAS traits. Although resource requirements are minimal I used the qos-500 facility on Hawk to get all the jobs through quickly.

Cell Types: 19 lineages (including sub-clusters like Glu-UL-0/1/2, GABA-0/1/2, etc.)
Functional Annotations: MaxCPP
Chromosomes: 22 autosomes
GWAS Traits: 5 neuropsychiatric disorders









Phase
Total Jobs
Complexity




make_annot_maxCPP
418
19 cell types X 1 Annotation X 22 Chrs


ldsr_ld_scores_hg38
418
Same as annotation phase above


ldsr_strat_hg38_bl_v12
95
19 cell types X 1 Annotation X 5 GWAS
